body.title: [sprint] system_alarms primitive — unignorable, deduped, self-clearing failure surfacing
body.intention: I intend that a should-not-happen failure drops an unignorable, deduped artifact instead of a log line lost in the noise.
body.description:
**Problem:** failures emit logs that drown — inference was dead for days, found by accident. Need a general 'system alarm' primitive: a log call that ALSO drops an unignorable artifact (the Number 5 Crossbar trouble-ticket model — nothing routine in this box).

**Shape:** A logging flag `system_alarm=True` (default False; loguru sink/patch on `extra`) drops a flat-file alarm at `~/.unseen_university/operations/system_alarms/<signature>.json` (path PRE-APPROVED). Flat-file NOT Postgres — a DB-down event must itself be alarmable, so the alarm can't depend on the DB. Dedup by **signature** (the subject, e.g. `no-provider:<tier>`, `specific-model:<model>`, `canary-failed:<provider>`): 2nd/Nth of the same signature increments, never a new file. Each alarm carries a **caller breakdown** `{caller: count}` (the fix-list) + aggregate count + first_seen/last_seen. On close, MOVE the file to `operations/system_alarms/archive/<signature>.json` (the 'is this chronic?' history). Self-clearing: a caller quiet past a window drops off the breakdown; when the breakdown empties the alarm ages out.

**Affected files:** new module under devices/ for the alarm dropper + a loguru sink/patch; reads/writes operations/system_alarms/ (+archive/). Canonical name system_alarm (NOT trouble-ticket/ttix). 
**Design rules:** flat-file (no-sqlite; alarm independent of Postgres); fail-soft (a broken alarm must never break the logger); log-the-crossing.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the drop/dedup/caller-breakdown/close-to-archive/self-clear mechanics + the `system_alarm=True` logging integration. OUT — notification (T-system-alarms-notify), the uu CLI (T-uu-alarms-cli), and any specific consumer.
**Test plan:** unit — dropping the same signature twice yields ONE file with count=2 and a 2-entry-or-merged caller breakdown; dropping a 2nd caller adds to the breakdown; close moves the file to archive/; a quiet caller is pruned and an emptied alarm ages out; `system_alarm=True` on a log call produces both the log line and the alarm file; the dropper swallows its own errors (logger still works when the alarm dir is unwritable).
**Completion criteria:** same-signature drops dedup to one file with correct aggregate + per-caller counts; close relocates to operations/system_alarms/archive/; `system_alarm=False`/absent drops NO file; alarm-drop failure never raises into the caller.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
